title: Incorrectly Identifying Articulation Points in a Graph with a Special Case

statement.markdown: Articulation points are the vertices in a graph from which removal would disconnect the graph. Given a connected undirected graph with n vertices and m edges, find all the articulation points in the graph.
statement.inputFormat: The first line of input contains two integers n and m representing the number of vertices and edges in the graph. Then m lines follow. Each line contains three integers u, v, and w representing an edge between two vertices u and v of weight w.
statement.outputFormat: Print all the articulation points in the graph.
statement.notes: If the graph is not connected, print nothing. The graph will have at least 2 vertices. The graph may contain self-loops or multiple edges between the same pair of vertices.
